name: Green Point Campground
location: Pacific Rim National Park Reserve — Long Beach unit
highlights: Only campground inside the Long Beach unit
Clifftop sites above the Pacific — ocean sound all night
Generator-free zone
Lighthouse loop 200 meters from the sites
bookingWindow: Reservations open in February at pc.gc.ca — fills in hours. Plan months ahead.
seasonalNotes: Seasonal — typically May through October. Winter camping not available.

name: Wya Point Resort
highlights: Owned and operated by the Yuułuʔiłʔatḥ First Nation
Oceanfront yurts, tent camping, and rustic lodging
600 acres of old-growth forest on an exclusive beach
Sleeping on land cared for by the Nation for 10,000 years
seasonalNotes: Year-round. One of the most distinctive and purposeful stays on the coast.

name: Clayoquot Wilderness Resort
location: Bedwell River, Clayoquot Sound — fly-in/boat-in only
highlights: Remote safari tents on the shores of Bedwell River
No road access — fly-in from Tofino or boat-in
All-inclusive: meals, kayaking, surfing, bear watching
Indigenous cultural experiences with Tla-o-qui-aht guides
Extreme privacy, extreme nature
seasonalNotes: Seasonal — typically May through September. The most remote stay in the region.

name: Pacific Sands Beach Resort
location: Tofino — Cox Bay (directly on surf break)
highlights: Directly on a consistent surf break at Cox Bay
Self-catering suites and cabins with fireplaces
Boards available, wetsuit rinse stations
Location is hard to beat for surfers
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Storm watching packages in winter. Surf is biggest September–November.

name: Long Beach Lodge Resort
highlights: Directly on the beach at Cox Bay
Dedicated surf camp operation
Well-regarded dining room (The Pointe Restaurant)
Great room with a fireplace the size of a car and Pacific views
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Storm watching from the great room is a winter tradition.

name: Pluvio Restaurant + Rooms
highlights: Six rooms above the best restaurant in Ucluelet
Small, thoughtfully furnished, quiet
Book room + dinner as a package
James Beard-nominated restaurant downstairs
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Six rooms total — intimate and hard to get. Book early.

name: Wickaninnish Inn
highlights: Defining luxury property on the outer coast of British Columbia
Every room faces the Pacific with floor-to-ceiling windows
On-beach access to Chesterman Beach
Ancient Cedars Spa
The Pointe Restaurant overlooking the ocean
One of the few truly world-class lodges in Canada
bookingWindow: Reserve 6–12 months ahead for peak season
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Storm watching season (Nov–Mar) is as compelling as summer — book the storm packages.
